basic_details.job_title: NCERT Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for Senior Research Associate and Junior Project Fellows Posts
basic_details.short_information: The NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026 notification has been released by the Department of Curriculum Studies & Development (DCS&D), National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT), New Delhi. NCERT will conduct a walk-in interview to engage one Senior Research Associate (SRA) and four Junior Project Fellows (JPFs) purely on a contractual basis under three PAC-approved programmes for the year 2026-27. The walk-in interview is scheduled for 15th July 2026 at Room No. 210, Second Floor, CIET Building, NCERT, New Delhi. This article covers complete details of vacancies, eligibility,

age_limit.details: Post Programme Age Limit SRA 7.01 Below 45 years (relaxation as per GoI norms) JPF 7.01 Below 40 years (relaxation as per GoI norms) JPF 7.03 Below 40 years (relaxation as per GoI norms) JPF 7.04 Below 40 years (relaxation as per GoI norms)

qualification.details: Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with a minimum of 55% marks for General category candidates and 50% marks for SC/ST/PH candidates. Experience: Two years' experience in the relevant field. The SRA must submit an attested copy of the salary slip/bank statement/ITR at the time of interview. Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic knowledge of computer, proficiency in MS Office, data processing and report preparation, proficiency in data analysis software, good communication skills, and experience in field work and data collection. | Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with minimum 55% marks (General) / 50% marks (SC/ST/PH). Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic computer knowledge, MS Office proficiency, data processing, field work and data collection experience, good communication skills. | Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with minimum 55% marks (General) / 50% marks (SC/ST/PH). Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic computer knowledge, MS Office proficiency, data processing, good communication skills. | Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with minimum 55% marks (General) / 50% marks (SC/ST/PH). Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic computer knowledge, MS Office proficiency, data processing, experience in translation of materials from English to Hindi and vice versa, good communication skills. | Note: Experience pertaining to Research/Teaching/Training/Extension Activities/Content Development should be related to School Education. Time spent by a candidate pursuing M.Phil and/or Ph.D. degree shall not count as teaching or research experience for eligibility purposes.

vacancy_details.Programme Title: Curricular and Pedagogical Practices for Holistic Development
Curricular and Pedagogical Practices for Holistic Development
Course Material for Master's Programme on Curriculum Development and Evaluation
Translation of Handbook on School Culture and Processes (NCF-SE 2023) in Hindi

post_wise_breakdown.programme_title: Curricular and Pedagogical Practices for Holistic Development
Curricular and Pedagogical Practices for Holistic Development

how_to_apply: There is no online application process for this recruitment. Interested and eligible candidates must follow these steps:
Download and fill the application form in the prescribed format provided with the official advertisement.
Attach self-attested copies of testimonials and experience certificates.
Carry original certificates and supporting documents for verification.
Report for registration at the Department of Curriculum Studies & Development, Room No. 210, Second Floor, CIET Building, NCERT, New Delhi, between 9:30 AM and 12:30 PM on 15th July 2026.
Appear for the walk-in interview on the same day for the respective post applied.
Note: No candidate will be entertained for registration after 12:30 PM.

content_sections.heading: NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: NCERT's Department of Curriculum Studies & Development is hiring for three research and development programmes. Below is a quick overview of the NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026.

rows.details: National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT)
One Senior Research Associate (SRA) and Four Junior Project Fellows (JPFs)
Room No. 210, Second Floor, CIET Building, NCERT, New Delhi

content_sections.heading: NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The recruitment covers three PAC-approved programmes with the following post distribution: One Senior Research Associate (SRA) One Junior Project Fellow (JPF) Two Junior Project Fellows (JPFs) One Junior Project Fellow (JPF)

rows.programme_title: Curricular and Pedagogical Practices for Holistic Development
Curricular and Pedagogical Practices for Holistic Development
Course Material for Master's Programme on Curriculum Development and Evaluation
Translation of Handbook on School Culture and Processes (NCF-SE 2023) in Hindi

content_sections.heading: NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must fulfil the eligibility conditions related to educational qualification, experience, and age limit as detailed below for each post.

content_sections.heading: NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026 Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with a minimum of 55% marks for General category candidates and 50% marks for SC/ST/PH candidates. Experience: Two years' experience in the relevant field. The SRA must submit an attested copy of the salary slip/bank statement/ITR at the time of interview. Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic knowledge of computer, proficiency in MS Office, data processing and report preparation, proficiency in data analysis software, good communication skills, and experience in field work and data collection. Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with minimum 55% marks (General) / 50% marks (SC/ST/PH). Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic computer knowledge, MS Office proficiency, data processing, field work and data collection experience, good communication skills. Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with minimum 55% marks (General) / 50% marks (SC/ST/PH). Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic computer knowledge, MS Office proficiency, data processing, good communication skills. Post Graduation in Mathematics/Science/Social Science/Language/Education with minimum 55% marks (General) / 50% marks (SC/ST/PH). Desirable: B.Ed./M.Ed., basic computer knowledge, MS Office proficiency, data processing, experience in translation of materials from English to Hindi and vice versa, good communication skills. Note: Experience pertaining to Research/Teaching/Training/Extension Activities/Content Development should be related to School Education. Time spent by a candidate pursuing M.Phil and/or Ph.D. degree shall not count as teaching or research experience for eligibility purposes.

faq.question: Q1. What is the NCERT SRA and JPF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: It is a walk-in interview recruitment drive by the Department of Curriculum Studies & Development, NCERT, to engage one Senior Research Associate and four Junior Project Fellows on a contractual basis for the year 2026-27.

faq.question: Q2. When is the NCERT walk-in interview scheduled?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview is scheduled for 15th July 2026 at Room No. 210, Second Floor, CIET Building, NCERT, New Delhi.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for the SRA post at NCERT?
faq.answer: The SRA post carries a consolidated remuneration of Rs. 42,000/- per month for NET/Ph.D. holders and Rs. 40,000/- per month for Non-NET candidates.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for the JPF post at NCERT?
faq.answer: Junior Project Fellows receive Rs. 37,000/- per month (consolidated) for NET/Ph.D. holders and Rs. 35,000/- per month for Non-NET candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for these posts?
faq.answer: The age limit is below 45 years for the SRA post and below 40 years for all JPF posts, with relaxation as per Government of India norms.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No application fee has been mentioned in the official advertisement, as selection is through a walk-in interview.

faq.question: Q7. What documents should candidates carry for the interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry the duly filled application form, self-attested testimonials, experience certificates, original certificates for verification, and an attested salary slip/bank statement/ITR where applicable.

faq.question: Q8. Is the NCERT SRA/JPF post permanent?
faq.answer: No, these posts are purely temporary and contractual, with tenure up to 31st March 2027 (or six months from joining for the Programme 7.04 JPF post).
